project: AI Credit Underwriting Platform — Alinma Bank
description: Build plan organized into two phases: enablers (foundation that every business story depends on) and the Personal Finance business story (the first product end-to-end). All enablers must be complete before the business story begins.

phases.name: Enablers
phases.description: Foundational tasks that have no business logic of their own but are prerequisites for any product story. Adding a second product later (Credit Cards, Auto Lease, Real Estate Mortgage) reuses everything in this phase unchanged.

tasks.title: Azure DevOps repository and OpenShift deployment integration
tasks.description: Establish the version-controlled home for the project and wire it to the bank's OpenShift deployment target. This is the foundation every other task builds on: without a repository, nothing can be committed; without a cluster connection, nothing can be deployed. Branch protection on main ensures no code reaches production without passing CI and a peer review.
acceptance_criteria: Repository exists in Azure DevOps and is accessible to the team
CI pipeline can authenticate to OpenShift and deploy a test workload
Merging to main requires a passing CI build and at least one approved PR review

tasks.title: Project bootstrap — dependencies, configuration, logging, and local infrastructure
tasks.description: Set up everything a developer needs to clone the repo and run the platform on their machine. This bundles four concerns that have no value individually but together form the development baseline: a Python project with locked dependencies (so every environment runs identical package versions), a typed configuration loader that reads environment variables and refuses to start if anything required is missing, a structured JSON logger emitting consistent correlation fields on every entry (so logs can be searched and filtered without manual parsing), and a docker-compose stack with Kafka (KRaft mode, no ZooKeeper) and Postgres reachable from inside the Docker network. Internal services are deliberately not exposed to the host, matching the production security posture where Kafka and the database are cluster-internal.
acceptance_criteria: Running the dependency install command produces a reproducible lock file
A template environment file documents every variable a developer must set
The application refuses to start with a clear error if a required configuration value is missing
All log output written to stdout is valid JSON with correlation fields (application_id, component, log level, timestamp) on every entry
docker-compose brings up Kafka (KRaft) and Postgres in one command, with the application topic auto-created
Postgres and Kafka are not reachable from the host machine — only from within the Docker network

tasks.title: Document Management System (DMS) mockup service
tasks.description: The real DMS is a bank system not available in local development or CI. This mockup provides the same HTTP surface as the real DMS — backed by the local filesystem — so the rest of the platform can be developed and tested without any dependency on bank infrastructure. Two operations are required to support every downstream story: uploading a new document and fetching an existing one by ID. Persistence across container restarts is mandatory; pre-seeded sample documents allow developers to run the full stack and see processing happen immediately, with no manual upload step.
acceptance_criteria: A document can be uploaded and later fetched by the ID returned at upload time
Documents persist across container restarts
Sample documents are pre-seeded on first startup so end-to-end runs work without manual setup

tasks.title: AI service client (OpenAI-compatible)
tasks.description: The platform calls an AI backend in two distinct contexts: per-document structured extraction (must be deterministic) and per-case narrative generation for the report (slight temperature for natural language). The client abstracts both call patterns behind a single interface, reads its endpoint, key, and model name entirely from environment configuration, and supports both OpenAI and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — so the bank can switch providers or move to an on-premise model without touching pipeline code. Structured output is enforced via pydantic schemas so the AI cannot return malformed responses that would crash the pipeline downstream.
acceptance_criteria: Document extraction returns a typed result that includes both the verification decision and all extracted fields in a single response
Narrative generation returns a coherent paragraph suitable for a credit assessment report
Endpoint, API key, and model name are read entirely from environment configuration — no hard-coded values

tasks.title: DMS service client
tasks.description: Every document the platform processes is fetched from and delivered to DMS. This client encapsulates that HTTP interaction — providing fetch and upload operations — so the pipeline never constructs raw HTTP calls. Keeping I/O behind a client interface also makes the pipeline testable: tests can substitute a fake client without spinning up a real DMS.
acceptance_criteria: A document can be fetched by its DMS ID and its binary content returned to the caller
A generated report can be uploaded and its DMS document ID returned to the caller
The DMS base URL is read from configuration, with no hard-coded addresses

tasks.title: Persistence layer — domain enums, database tables, async session, migrations
tasks.description: Before any business logic or pipeline can be written, the system needs a shared vocabulary (product types, document types, case and document statuses, validation outcomes, recommendations) and the persistence shape that pipeline writes will follow. The enums become the contract between every layer of the system. The tables encode the design's hard requirements: pipeline tables are append-only (rows are never updated after insert, so the audit trail is tamper-evident); the audit event table is partitioned by month to keep query performance stable as years of events accumulate; the EDW staging table stores a JSONB snapshot per case alongside indexed scalar columns so the workbench can filter without joining across pipeline tables. Migrations are managed by Alembic so schema changes are versioned and applied consistently across environments.
acceptance_criteria: All product types, document types, and statuses defined in the BRD are represented as enums and referenced by every layer that needs them
Running the migration command from a clean database creates all tables, indexes, foreign keys, and partition definitions
Audit event partitions are pre-created so inserts do not fail when a new month begins
An async session factory is available for the pipeline; no module constructs its own session or engine

tasks.title: Base abstractions — product handler and document extraction schema
tasks.description: Two abstract base classes define the contracts every product and every document type will conform to. The product handler base declares the two methods every product must implement (which documents are required for this applicant, and how to validate them) and owns the responsibility of stamping the active config version onto every validation result — so a future handler author cannot forget to record what thresholds were in effect when a decision was made. The document extraction base enforces the platform's single-call verify-and-extract contract: a naive integration would make two AI calls per document (one to verify, one to extract), doubling latency and cost. The base class makes that mistake structurally impossible — subclasses cannot break the single-call invariant because the interface does not support it. Every extracted field is wrapped in a typed envelope carrying a confidence score that downstream rules can read for low-confidence flagging.
acceptance_criteria: Adding a new product requires only a new subclass and a registry entry — no changes to the pipeline or the base class
Every validation result produced by any handler carries the active config version automatically, without the handler author having to remember to add it
Processing any document type requires exactly one AI call, never two
Every field on every extraction schema carries a confidence score readable without unwrapping or casting

phases.name: Personal Finance Business Story
phases.description: The first and currently only product in scope. With the enablers in place, the Personal Finance story is implemented as a vertical slice that fulfils the four document-validation user stories — UN-01 (document completeness), UN-02 (national ID / Iqama validation), UN-03 (salary certificate validation), UN-04 (salary transfer letter validation) — through real Kafka, real DMS, and real AI calls. Adding subsequent products (Credit Cards, Auto Lease, Real Estate Mortgage) will follow this same shape, reusing every enabler unchanged.

user_stories.story: As a credit underwriting reviewer, I want the system to validate for each case that all mandatory documents are present and correct in the system, so I can confirm document completeness before reviewing.
user_stories.criteria: All required documents per product and employer class are listed; missing documents are flagged with a clear 'Missing' label; unexpected or unsupported documents are flagged separately; result with reason displayed as Complete / Missing / Extra / Not compliant.

user_stories.story: As a credit underwriting reviewer, I want the system to validate certain fields inside the customer national ID/Iqama document against system fields, so I can confirm identity consistency.
user_stories.criteria: Fields compared: ID number, name. For non-Saudi customers validation is mandatory. For Saudi customers validation is also mandatory and the case must record whether the ID has been OTP-verified. Result per field: Match / Mismatch / Review Manually.

user_stories.story: As a credit underwriting reviewer, I want the system to validate certain fields inside the salary certificate document against system fields, so I can confirm salary data accuracy.
user_stories.criteria: Fields validated: customer name, ID number, employer name, job title, basic salary, net salary, employment date. Each field compared between document and CRM/T24. Result per field: Matching / Mismatch / Review Manually.

user_stories.story: As a credit underwriting reviewer, I want the system to validate certain fields inside the salary transfer letter against system fields, so I can confirm salary transfer accuracy.
user_stories.criteria: Fields validated: customer name, ID number, employer name, bank transfer name (Al Inmaa), account/IBAN number. Each field compared between document and system. Result per field: Matching / Mismatch / Review Manually.

tasks.title: Document extraction schemas — national ID/Iqama, salary certificate, salary transfer letter
tasks.description: Each document the bank processes has a unique set of fields and its own rules for what makes it valid. The AI needs document-specific instructions to extract these correctly — generic prompts produce unreliable results. This task produces one extraction schema per document type required by the four user stories, with tailored verification criteria (what disqualifies the document) and field-level extraction guidance (date formats, currency conventions, IBAN normalisation). The schemas are the contract between the AI and the validation rules: every field that UN-02 / UN-03 / UN-04 specify must be extractable from the corresponding document. National ID / Iqama (UN-02) requires ID number, name, and expiry; the schema must also expose nationality so the handler can branch Saudi vs non-Saudi at validation time. Salary certificate (UN-03) requires customer name, ID number, employer name, job title, basic salary, net salary, employment date. Salary transfer letter (UN-04) requires customer name, ID number, employer name, the receiving bank name (which the handler will check matches 'Al Inmaa'), and the account / IBAN number. A central registry resolves DocumentType to schema class so the AI client and the product handler always look up the same schema for the same document type.
acceptance_criteria: An extraction schema exists for national ID / Iqama, salary certificate, and salary transfer letter
Every field listed in UN-02, UN-03, UN-04 acceptance criteria is present on the corresponding schema, named consistently, and typed to match what the validation rule expects
The national ID / Iqama schema exposes nationality so Saudi vs non-Saudi branching can be applied
Every document type defined in the domain enum is resolvable through the registry; an unregistered type raises a named exception immediately, not a silent failure

tasks.title: Personal Finance product handler — completeness check, per-field validations, tunable thresholds
tasks.description: The handler encodes every Personal Finance business rule across the four user stories. UN-01 is satisfied by required_documents(applicant_data), which returns the set of documents this applicant must present (varies by employer class A/B/C/D/GOV per the BRD matrix) plus the set of supported types. The pipeline uses the result to flag missing required documents AND unexpected/unsupported uploads, producing the Complete / Missing / Extra / Not compliant outcome the story specifies. UN-02 produces per-field results for ID number and name comparison against the CRM payload, with branching on nationality: for non-Saudi applicants validation is mandatory; for Saudi applicants validation is also mandatory and the result records whether the ID has been OTP-verified upstream (a flag the CRM event carries) so the reviewer sees that context. UN-03 produces seven per-field results for the salary certificate (customer name, ID number, employer name, job title, basic salary, net salary, employment date), each compared against the CRM/T24 snapshot embedded in applicant_data. UN-04 produces per-field results for the salary transfer letter (customer name, ID number, employer name, bank transfer name verified to be 'Al Inmaa', account / IBAN). Every per-field rule emits Matching, Mismatch, or Review Manually — Review Manually is reserved for low-confidence extractions where the AI's confidence falls below the configured threshold. Numeric thresholds (confidence cutoffs, salary deviation tolerance) and the outcome-to-recommendation mapping live in a versioned YAML file so risk teams can tune them without a code deployment; the active version is stamped onto every persisted validation result, so the audit trail can always reproduce which thresholds were in effect when a decision was made — a hard regulatory requirement.
acceptance_criteria: UN-01: completeness check produces one of Complete / Missing / Extra / Not compliant. Missing required documents and unexpected/unsupported uploads are both flagged on the case
UN-01: required document set matches the BRD's employer-class matrix for each employer class (A/B/C/D/GOV)
UN-02: ID number and name produce per-field Match / Mismatch / Review Manually outcomes
UN-02: For Saudi applicants, the case records whether the ID has been OTP-verified; validation runs in both Saudi and non-Saudi cases
UN-03: customer name, ID number, employer name, job title, basic salary, net salary, employment date each produce a Matching / Mismatch / Review Manually outcome compared against CRM/T24
UN-04: customer name, ID number, employer name, bank transfer name (verified as 'Al Inmaa'), account / IBAN each produce a Matching / Mismatch / Review Manually outcome
Salary deviation tolerance is read from validation_config.yaml, not a hard-coded number
The processor refuses to start and prints a clear error if any required threshold key is missing from the config file
Every persisted validation result carries the config version that was loaded at the time
Changing a threshold and restarting the processor applies the new threshold to subsequent cases — with no code change

tasks.title: End-to-end pipeline — Kafka consumer, orchestration, report generation, sample applicants
tasks.description: This is what wires every enabler into a working product. A long-running processor consumes Kafka events and drives each application through the full lifecycle: deduplicate (so a Kafka redelivery does not double-process), create or load the case, fetch every document from DMS concurrently, run the AI verify-and-extract per document, check completeness against the handler's required documents (UN-01), run the handler's per-field validation rules (UN-02 / UN-03 / UN-04), and persist the recommendation. Once business processing is committed, the report generator produces the deliverable: an AI-written narrative paragraph opens the report, source documents are re-fetched from DMS and embedded directly into the PDF as base64 (so the PDF is fully self-contained), the HTML is rendered from a Jinja2 template and persisted independently in the database (so the PDF can be regenerated without re-running the pipeline if rendering produces a corrupt file), the PDF is rendered, and the PDF is uploaded back to DMS. A CRM mockup ships with sample applicants chosen to demonstrate each user story end-to-end: a happy-path case where every document and every field passes, a missing-document case (UN-01 Missing), an extra-document case (UN-01 Extra), a Saudi vs non-Saudi pair (UN-02 branching), a salary-mismatch case (UN-03 Mismatch on basic salary), and a transfer-letter mismatch case (UN-04 Mismatch on IBAN). Startup recovery marks any case left in a non-terminal state by a previous run as FAILED so operators can resubmit. The pipeline's completed_at timestamp is only written after the PDF upload succeeds, enforcing the platform's terminal-state guarantee: a COMPLETED case always has a delivered report. Technical exceptions (infrastructure failures, AI timeouts) are captured with a visually distinct treatment in the PDF — a regulatory requirement so a credit officer cannot mistake a system error for a deliberate DECLINE.
acceptance_criteria: A sample event exists and runs end-to-end for each user story scenario: happy path, UN-01 Missing, UN-01 Extra, UN-02 Saudi, UN-02 non-Saudi, UN-03 salary mismatch, UN-04 IBAN mismatch
Each sample case produces the expected recommendation and a PDF uploaded to DMS
Redelivering the same Kafka message twice results in exactly one processed case
Cases left in CREATED or IN_PROGRESS when the processor restarts are marked FAILED with an explanatory message
completed_at is written only after the PDF upload to DMS succeeds — never before
The generated PDF can be opened with no external references (images, fonts, source document previews are all embedded)
The HTML used to produce the PDF is stored in the database independently, so the PDF can be regenerated without reprocessing
The technical exceptions panel in the PDF is rendered with a visual treatment clearly distinct from the validation outcomes panel
